rag_rules: Assumptions and guarantees for component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Implementation-level bindings and connections should be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections are defined.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and feature names from the component type when defining assumptions and guarantees.
Ensure that component type contracts only reference ports through valid variables or outputs.
Place annex blocks in scopes that match the level of abstraction of the contained elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees using named constants or visible feature relations.
Implementation scopes must define bindings and connections after subcomponent declarations.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type contracts.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type annexes without proper variable representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex syntax and validator-driven repair patterns
Model analyst focus on AADL structural extraction
